title: Strategic Pivot – Fortune 500 Autonomous Coding System
principle: Quality over speed. Ship perfect or never.

key_conflicts.roadmap_position: Python/LangGraph and Python linters in SSOT v1.1 and Atomic_Phased_Systemgap_plan
key_conflicts.resolution: Implement multi-agent orchestration and quality gates in Node; map Python-centric tools to Node equivalents

key_conflicts.roadmap_position: React/Next.js UI in spec_for_autonomy and taxonomy-2
key_conflicts.cdi_constraint: Frontend must be vanilla JS/CSS under /public
key_conflicts.resolution: Deliver chat and progress UI in vanilla JS; defer React until a stack change proposal is approved

key_conflicts.cdi_constraint: No new dependencies without explicit justification
key_conflicts.resolution: Introduce queue abstraction behind existing StepQueue; allow BullMQ backend when justified; keep default inline

key_conflicts.roadmap_position: CycloneDX, SLSA, SIEM, ASVS, EU AI Act mappings
key_conflicts.resolution: Add minimal, testable evidence tracks (SBOM exists), plan SLSA provenance and SIEM logs as incremental wins

options_assessed.name: Continue current contracts
options_assessed.notes: Steady but too slow for F500 bar

options_assessed.name: Evidence-based hybrid
options_assessed.notes: Reconcile roadmaps with CDI; incremental, testable wins

recommendation.summary: Adopt an evidence-based hybrid: implement multi-agent seams inside current Node CDI, add chat interaction, strengthen trust & compliance spine (SBOM→SLSA, SIEM), and keep UI vanilla.
why: Aligns with ai-stack.json and protects current contracts
Delivers measurable risk reduction and auditability
Prepares for scale without big-bang rewrites

wins: Convert SBOM to CycloneDX 1.6 or produce both
Add RFC 9457 error envelopes in API responses
Introduce action logs → .telemetry and SIEM-friendly JSONL
Model RA/AA/SA/IA/QA/DBA/DA as StepQueue step types
Add plan payloads and evidence outputs per step
Vanilla JS chat under /public with SSE

risks.mitigation: Gate with CDI contracts and per-win evidence
Vanilla JS chat now; RFC to change stack later
Keep defaults inline; make MQ/queue optional backend
